name: Dependable Medical Transport
recent_news: Litigation history: Ganley v. Dependable Medical Transport Services LLC (Ariz. App. 2015) over promissory note repayment - indicates prior financial distress; no recent M&A news.
fit_notes: Small NEMT/medical transport LLC in Glendale AZ - appears to be non-emergency medical (van) transport, not school/SPED bus contractor. Weak fit for Summit's K-12 school bus tuck-in strategy; ~$1.4M revenue and prior financial issues make it sub-scale.

name: After Hours Limousine Svc
recent_news: No M&A or notable news found; website unreachable.
fit_notes: Lowell MA limo service tagged as special-needs in source data but public footprint shows traditional limousine/livery, not school district contracts. Sub-scale ($1.2M, 12 employees) and unclear school-bus relevance - weak Summit tuck-in fit unless it holds school SPED van routes.

name: Sandollar Limousine Svc
recent_news: No school-bus or M&A news; not to be confused with same-named Sandollar Limo in Louisville KY (separate entity).
fit_notes: Athens AL PO Box-based small limo/charter operation; no evidence of school district or SPED contracts. The website sandollarlimo.com actually points to a Louisville KY charter operator - unclear whether Athens AL entity is independent or related. Sub-scale and out-of-category for Summit's K-12 school bus tuck-in focus.

name: Educational Bus Transportation
recent_news: 2025: Zenobe and EBT partnered to deploy 10 electric school buses for Copiague Public Schools - first-in-service electric school bus in NY State; 3rd-gen Sean Corr named President of EBT.
fit_notes: Highly attractive but very large target - EBT is a subsidiary of family-owned Trans Group (Corr family, 3rd gen) with 1,300+ vehicles, 2,000+ employees across NY/Long Island; recently named new president and just made major EV investment, signaling reinvestment rather than near-term sale. Would be a platform-scale acquisition, not a tuck-in; succession risk low given active 3rd-gen leadership.

name: Krapf School Bus
principals: Dale N. Krapf (Chairman of the Board)
districts_or_customers: Multiple districts in PA, DE, NJ, NY, VA (including West Chester area, Downingtown, Honey Brook); operates transit routes in NY (Lewis, Madison, Oneida, Otsego counties via Birnie Bus)
recent_news: 2017: acquired Birnie Bus Service (Rome, NY); prior acquisitions of Septran Inc.; continues organic and acquisitive growth; 75th anniversary celebrated 2017 - still 3rd-gen family-led.
fit_notes: One of the largest privately-owned/family-owned school bus contractors in the US (2,500+ vehicles, 3,500+ employees, ~$250M revenue, 13 locations across PA/DE/NJ/NY/VA). Family is acquisitive (Birnie, Septran) rather than a seller; would be a platform-level transaction or strategic combination - not a typical Summit tuck-in. Low near-term succession signal with active 3rd/4th-gen family leadership (Blake as CEO).
